title: Updating State Based on Previous State
description: Learn the safe way to update state when the new value depends on the old one.

slides.title: The Problem with Direct Updates ⚠️
The Functional Update Pattern 🔄
slides.content:
## Using a Function in setState

The setter function can also accept a **callback** that receives the previous state as an argument. This guarantees you're working with the latest state.

```jsx
setCount(prevCount => prevCount + 1);
```

Now, if you call `increment` three times in a row, it will correctly increase by 3 because each update receives the most recent state.

This pattern is essential when the new state depends on the previous state.

slides.title: Counter with Functional Update
slides.content: Here's the counter using the safe pattern:

quizData.question: When should you use the functional update pattern (e.g., `setCount(prev => prev + 1)`)?

options.text: Only when the new state depends on the previous state

quizData.explanation: Use functional updates when the new value is computed from the previous state to avoid stale closures.
